retry_reason: Failure from allocation (kzalloc) is not transient under the same conditions; retrying would likely fail again.
contract_summary: Get-or-create wrapper: returns a pointer to a batadv_orig_node_vlan with an acquired reference (refcount incremented). If the object already exists, its reference is taken via batadv_orig_node_vlan_get. If not, a new object is allocated, its refcount is initialized to 1 via kref_init and then incremented again (to 2) so both the list and the caller hold a reference. The caller must later release the returned reference with a corresponding put function (e.g., batadv_orig_node_vlan_put). The returned pointer may be NULL if the vid is invalid or memory allocation fails; the caller must check for NULL.

The segment count (3) matches the number of types in the chain (3).
